name: Gilyak

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Amur Nivkh (gily1242).

notes: Skeleton. Nivkh (Gilyak) is a Paleosiberian language isolate; this entry follows the Amur dialect Cyrillic alphabet. Beyond the base Russian letters it covers the language-specific graphemes for the rich dorsal system: Ӈ (velar nasal), the velar/uvular fricatives Ғ (ɣ) and Ӷ/Ҕ (ʁ), the uvular stops Ӄ/Қ and Ҟ, the uvular fricative Ҳ (χ) and the voiceless sonorant Ҏ. Nivkh contrasts aspirated and unaspirated stops and undergoes extensive initial-consonant mutation; the aspiration values assigned here are approximate and the morphophonemic alternations are not recoverable from spelling, so a raised PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Amur Nivkh (gily1242)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
